Now this particular module is called creating customers for life. You can do everything it takes to master the straight line system and perfect your inter-game of success as well. But if you don't end up keeping the sales you've made, or better still turning those sales into additional sales, which includes generating a consistent stream of referrals from your customers, then you're making your life a hell of a lot harder than it has to be. So to give all the things I can teach you to ramp up your sales results, the easiest way for me to teach you to put a whole lot more money in your pocket and to have a whole lot less stress in your life while you're doing it, is to learn how to first hold on to your customers once you close them so you don't get cancellations, and then second, take as many of those customers as possible and build them into long-term relationships, or as the phrase goes, create customers for life. Now if you haven't been the slightest bit of experience either in business or as a sales person, then you're painfully aware of how much it costs to get a new customer and how much energy it takes in all levels. Everything from having to develop a kick-ass marketing campaign to sitting through the leads to capturing someone's attention on the phone to actually getting yourself in front of the person to make your presentation, essentially all that stuff that has to happen before you even have a shot of the prospect saying yes is typically the most expensive aspect of any company's business. And in fact, in a lot of cases, it's what ends up driving a company out of business. So what this module is about is providing you with some really powerful yet really simple strategies that help you get a ton more sales from your existing customer base, and if necessary, stop any bleeding you're currently experiencing in the form of customer charm. Now in straight-line terminology, the way you go about doing this is by making sure that once you get your prospect to move past their action threshold and you get them to buy, you need to make sure that first you keep them past their action threshold over time, and I'll tell you how to do that in a moment, and then second, I want you to learn to actually permanently lower their action threshold, albeit only as it relates to you. In other words, when they deal with other sales, people with their action threshold will still be way up there, right? But when they deal with you, their action threshold will be much, much lower. And the way you do that is to turn them into raving fans of these three things. And by the way, it should come as no surprise to you what these three things are. They're the three tens. You want to turn them into raving fans of your product. You want to turn them into raving fans of you, and you want to turn them into raving fans of your company, the three tens. Now for starters, this means making sure that you keep track and keep all your promises. Essentially, you follow through and you treat your customers with the highest level of ethics integrity at all times. Now there are three things we're going to cover in this session. First we'll go over what you can do immediately after the sale to lock things in and set the stage for a lifelong relationship. Two, then I'll actually walk you through strategies for getting customers for life, strategies you can and should use as part of your everyday sales process. And by the way, how you handle your interactions after the sale affects your reputation as a sales person in a massive way. I'll tell you, back on my Wall Street days we had this saying. It said, good news travels fast, but bad news travels instantly. And that was before the internet. Nowadays, bad news not only travels instantly, but it travels virally. You get it? And that means the reputation as a sales person is exactly the case. You develop a better reputation of how you treat customers after the sale. You're totally, totally screwed. And all it takes is one angry customer to start blogging about you and you'll find yourself either on one of those reputation management firms that specialize in removing negative Google posts, right? And they really don't work anyway. You'll be heading down to the local courthouse to change your name. Okay? You get it? Not what you want to be doing. Your reputation is something that's really tough to change once you've been established. So you want to make sure that it gets built in a way that supports you. Now finally, the third thing is I'll share some strategies to help you build a massive referral business. And that means lowering your lead cost dramatically. In other words, if you're getting referrals, you don't have to go out and buy leads and typically those will be more qualified leads and a bit easier to close and we'll explain that later, okay? So essentially, by making sure that your customers are satisfied at the highest possible level, it'll pay you back tenfold on so many different levels and throughout your entire sales career. Okay? So that's it. Thank you. Bye. Bye. Bye. Bye. Bye.
